description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a wooden fixed stock and foregrip, a blued steel receiver, and a prominent round drum magazine. The receiver and barrel are metallic, with a ribbed cooling section along the barrel and a simple cylindrical muzzle. The wooden components, including the stock and foregrip, have a rich brown finish and ergonomic shaping for comfortable handling. The drum magazine is large and circular, positioned beneath the receiver, and is characteristic of early 20th-century firearm designs. Iron sights are mounted on the top of the receiver and barrel, providing basic aiming capability. The overall construction combines wood and metal, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A classic submachine gun with a wooden stock and foregrip, blued steel receiver, and a large round drum magazine. Features iron sights and a ribbed barrel for cooling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and iron sights.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the rifle, wooden stock, foregrip, and the prominent drum magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the stock and the top of the drum magazine.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden components, receiver, and drum magazine from the other side.
